patterns_recognized: 7 of 8 games today collapse to an effective SKIP through hard caps (CAP1, CAP7, CAP10) or a clean natural D-grade dual-opposing-signal pattern; only COL @ LAD required manual intervention beyond confirming the pipeline's own caps.

COL @ LAD (pick_prob=0.8766) clears HARD CAP 9's >0.80 threshold, but grade_reasons shows only CAP 4 fired (Stage1/2 delta 0.24, score 3->1) — CAP 9 never applied. This is the same rule-engine miss the postgame archive flagged on 2026-06-03 (TOR@ATL). Applying CAP 9 manually as a DOWNGRADE, backed by its 3-for-3 May validation (ATL@LAD, SF@LAD x2) plus 2026-06-20 PIT@COL (Skenes 82.2%, lost).

TOR @ SF repeats the CAP 7 pick-side-bullpen-disadvantage pattern (0-for-7 backtest, incl. 2026-05-10 PIT@SF) on an otherwise elite pre-cap A/8 stack (Roupp -0.34 xERA edge over Gausman). Archive shows re-elevating capped PLATINUM/DIAMOND picks has blown up repeatedly this season (2026-06-04 TOR@ATL, 2026-06-20/21 BAL@LAD both lost after being played past a cap) — confirming the cap rather than trusting the strong underlying signal.

Internal grade_reasons text on two rows quotes a pick_prob that doesn't match today's CSV column (HOU@WSH logged 0.527 vs actual 0.5067; TOR@SF logged 0.535 vs actual 0.5642) — the cap firings themselves are still treated as authoritative per standing instructions, but the drift suggests the grading pass ran on a slightly earlier data snapshot than the final CSV, worth a pipeline check outside this review.

The postgame archive has a 15-day gap (last file 2026-06-21, none 2026-06-22 through 2026-07-05), so every June lesson cited here — CAP1's tightening trend, the recurring CAP9 pipeline miss, and CAP10's contested CONFIRM-exception being retired — is running on data now roughly two weeks stale.

PHI @ KC.reasoning: HARD CAP 1 fires on a -18.13pp negative edge on a PLATINUM pick (score 3->1), then HARD CAP 10 stacks (pick_prob 0.5067 in the bottom bucket, no CONFIRM) forcing score to 0. Archive shows CAP1 has only tightened this season -- 2026-06-04 TOR@ATL and 2026-06-20/21 BAL@LAD both blew up when a CAP1-capped tier was allowed to play at full strength. Sanchez's fair_prob (0.688) vs model (0.507) suggests the market already prices Philly's edge -- this reads as calibrator lag, not opportunity. Confirming the double cap.

NYY @ TB.reasoning: Clean natural D-grade with no cap needed: Jax's 2.23-xERA disadvantage vs Schlittler and PQI both oppose TB while Stage 1/2 agree tightly. pick_prob (0.5995) sits well outside CAP10's (0.50,0.55] band, so this is the model's own stack rejecting the pick, not a forced cap. Archive treats this dual-opposing D pattern as a coin-flip rather than a fade cue, so no override toward NYY either. Confirming SKIP on both sides.
lessons_applied: dual-opposing D-grade is uninformative, not a fade signal

HOU @ WSH.reasoning: Modest pre-cap signal (PQI against WSH, Delta=-5.1; Stage1/2 agree Delta=0.10) gets held at score 0 by HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob 0.5067, bottom bucket, no CONFIRM). bullpen_fatigue/confidence_downgrade flags are present but archive precedent treats stress flags as stake-size signals, not directional. Platoon/BvP top-3 on both sides is mostly SMALL_SAMPLE or NO_DATA with no collective OWNER/WEAK_VS pattern. Confirming SKIP.

NYM @ ATL.reasoning: Naturally graded D/-3, no cap needed: Lopez (ATL) grades 0.84 xERA worse than Peralta and PQI opposes ATL (Delta=-9.9) despite Stage1/2 agreeing closely. acute_roster/bullpen_fatigue flags present but treated as stake-size-only per archive, not directional. BvP shows only one collective note -- Lindor (NYM) is WEAK_VS today's SP at 27 PA -- which if anything mildly reinforces fading NYM rather than flipping the pick. Confirming SKIP.

MIL @ STL.reasoning: No hard cap needed -- edge_pp is +4.62, inside the normal band. SP edge (Dustin May, MIL) 0.52 xERA against STL and PQI also opposes STL (Delta=-7.4) while Stage1/2 agree almost exactly (Delta=0.00). Clean natural D/-2. Archive treats this signal combination as a coin-flip rather than a directional fade, so not flipping to MIL. Confirming SKIP.

ARI @ SD.reasoning: SP edge (Buehler, SD) 0.48 xERA against ARI and PQI also opposes ARI (Delta=+8.9), with Stage1/2 agreeing exactly (Delta=0.00). Natural D/-2, no hard cap needed. Archive shows Buehler's June results with SD were mixed (contributor in a win 06-08, on the losing side 06-20/21), not a clear override signal. bullpen_fatigue/confidence_downgrade flags present but non-directional per archive. Confirming SKIP.

TOR @ SF.reasoning: Pre-cap this was an elite A/8 (Roupp's 0.34-xERA edge over Gausman, who now pitches for TOR per the away_sp_name field; F-signal fires; Stage1/2 agree Delta=0.01), but HARD CAP 7 fires on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0063 (SF's high-leverage pen is the worse one), then CAP 10 stacks -- score collapses 8->1->0. CAP7's backtest is 0-for-7 including a prior SF loss (2026-05-10 PIT@SF, 6-7); it's a validated bright-line rule, not magnitude-scaled. Confirming both caps over a strong pre-cap stack.
lessons_applied: CAP7 pick-side bullpen disadvantage 0-for-7 incl. PIT@SF 05-10

COL @ LAD.reasoning: pick_prob=0.8766 clears the HARD CAP 9 threshold (>0.80), a bucket validated 3-for-3 in May (ATL@LAD, SF@LAD x2) plus 2026-06-20 PIT@COL (Skenes 82.2%, lost). But grade_reasons here shows only CAP 4 firing (Stage1/2 Delta=0.24 + confidence_downgrade=True, score 3->1) -- CAP 9 never applied despite clearly exceeding 0.80, the same rule-engine miss the archive flagged 2026-06-03 on TOR@ATL. Downgrading to SKIP to apply CAP 9's intent manually since the pipeline itself did not enforce it here.
lessons_applied: CAP9 top-bucket hallucination applied manually (0.877>0.80; not triggered in grade_reasons)
